title: NoPE
summary: Decoder stacks that omit explicit positional embeddings and rely on causal attention structure and training signal to infer order.
openingSummary: NoPE (no positional embeddings) removes added position vectors, rotary transforms, and attention position biases—letting causal masking and learned layer dynamics carry order information instead.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: NoPE refers to transformer decoders trained without conventional positional injection—no absolute tables, rotary Q/K rotation, or ALiBi-style bias. Causal attention still prevents tokens from seeing the future, and depth-wise mixing can weakly encode order. The design tests how much explicit position machinery modern language modeling actually requires.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Position ablation papers and some efficient-architecture experiments highlight NoPE as a baseline contrast to RoPE-first stacks. When a config omits position parameters entirely, throughput and memory change slightly, but length generalization behavior can diverge sharply—worth noting before assuming a missing position table is a bug.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: A causal decoder might feed raw token embeddings directly into attention with only a lower-triangular mask. Without rotary bases or bias terms, the model must recover word order from activation patterns across layers rather than a dedicated position channel.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: NoPE is not the same as removing causality—causal masking still enforces autoregressive order. It is also not automatically better than RoPE; results depend on scale and training recipe. Finally, checkpoint inspection showing zero position weights does not prove NoPE unless rotary or bias paths are also absent.
